purpose: Executable, sequenced, and auditable formula/rule library for the Sustentra NY Part 253 Scope 1 demo. Version 0.3.1 integrates the detailed formula audit, recurring-issue audit summary, and product-team Formula Logic Review while preserving the v0.3 pipeline-safe architecture.

scope_boundary.primary_demo_scope: Scope 1 stationary combustion happy path for natural gas utility bills, with related validation/reconciliation/gap logic and phase-2 records for mobile, refrigerants, wastewater/AD, biomass/biogas, CEMS/process, energy input/output, and product data.
not_included: Complete implementation of all NY Part 253 source categories outside current demo resources
scope_boundary.important_design_rule: LLMs may classify/extract; deterministic backend code should execute formula calculations, validation checks, reconciliation, and gap trigger logic.

sources.role: J2 evidence types, minimum acceptance fields, assertions, evidence roles, gap trigger vocabulary.
Engineering workflow, normalized activity data, reconciliation engine, GapTicket schema, June 24 demo scope.
Scope 1 evidence extraction, calculation, validation, reconciliation, materiality screening.
Scope 1 category mapping and methodology validation rules.
Official NY Part 253 thresholds, source categories, recordkeeping, missing data, verification requirements.

sources.file_name: NY Part 253 Scope 1 Formula Audit Report.pdf
sources.role: Formula-by-formula revisions, trigger/input requirements, and regulatory citations.

sources.file_name: Audit Summary_NY Part 253 Scope 1 Formula Audit Report.docx
sources.role: Cross-library design rules for trigger detectability, pipeline sequencing, input origins, citations, and regulatory branches.
Product-team review emphasizing exact citations, external/internal basis separation, practical triggers, and NY-vs-federal GWP separation.

formula_record_schema.regulatory_basis: External regulatory citations only. No assurance standards or internal product dependencies.
formula_record_schema.assurance_basis: ISO/ISSA assurance standards or concepts supporting the control.
formula_record_schema.internal_basis: Internal evidence library, registries, configuration, or workflow dependencies.
formula_record_schema.represented_assertions: Assertions represented by internal routing/ticket rules without re-performing the upstream test.

formula_records.formula_logic: Parse raw quantity and unit; standardize number format and unit label.
revision_summary: Added unit registry / source-category unit triggers and quantity positivity readiness check.
Added explicit unit-registry and normalization-rule inputs so unit and traceability triggers can execute.

formula_records.formula_logic: Convert fuel activity into the calculation unit required by the selected method/factor.
revision_summary: Added calculation tier and fuel quantity source as required inputs; added Table C-1 and natural gas billing unit triggers.
Added explicit tier/billing-unit branch model and upstream dependency on raw quantity normalization.

branch_conditions.condition: fuel_type is natural gas and fuel_unit is therms
branch_conditions.action: use the therm billing-record conversion path required by the selected tier/method

branch_conditions.condition: fuel_type is natural gas and fuel_unit is MMBtu
branch_conditions.action: retain or normalize energy quantity without applying a therm conversion

branch_conditions.condition: standardized fuel type is not represented in Table C-1
branch_conditions.action: block default conversion and route to method review

formula_records.formula_logic: Normalize evidence dates and detect out-of-period or cross-year records.
revision_summary: Added allocation method support, explicit end-date missing trigger, allocation-method-missing trigger, and period sequence checks.
Added allocation basis reference for cross-year proration traceability.

branch_conditions.condition: period_start and period_end fall in different reporting years
branch_conditions.action: require allocation_method and allocation_basis_document_reference before downstream use

formula_records.formula_logic: Match evidence to facility registry using address, facility name, account/meter/vendor identifiers, or auditor mapping.
formula_records.notes: Validation output, not purely raw extraction.
revision_summary: Added account/meter matching input, low-confidence mapping and operational-control triggers, and renamed multi-facility trigger.
Added confidence-threshold, operational-control, and multi-facility split inputs required by its gap triggers.

formula_records.formula_logic: Match evidence to declared source/equipment registry and determine whether source is active/in scope.
revision_summary: Added source mapping confidence, portable equipment exclusion, and combustion unit-type trigger/input.
Added confidence-threshold, portability, and source-status inputs required by source-mapping triggers.

formula_records.regulatory_scope: Data quality and missing/substituted data handling
formula_records.formula_logic: Classify record as actual, estimated, substituted, or unknown; require method support where needed.
revision_summary: Added data capture rate, substitution capture-rate and mislabeled actual/estimated triggers; replaced readiness checks and added cutoff assertion.
Added estimation/substitution method references and evidence actuality indicator so classification triggers can execute.

branch_conditions.condition: evidence context demonstrates measured/billed/observed data
branch_conditions.action: classify ACTUAL

branch_conditions.condition: value is estimated and estimation method is documented
branch_conditions.action: classify ESTIMATED

branch_conditions.condition: value replaces missing/invalid data under a missing-data method
branch_conditions.action: classify SUBSTITUTED and retain capture-rate/method support

formula_records.formula_logic: Reject or reclassify purchased electricity from Scope 1 combustion unless tied to onsite fuel/cogeneration logic.
revision_summary: Clarified regulatory basis vs internal J2-002 routing support; added electricity role input and onsite cogeneration/electricity-as-Scope-1 triggers.
Added cogeneration/onsite-generation context required by exception triggers.

branch_conditions.condition: electricity is purchased/imported energy
branch_conditions.action: exclude from Scope 1 combustion activity

branch_conditions.condition: electricity evidence is linked to onsite cogeneration
branch_conditions.action: retain as energy-output support while calculating Scope 1 from the associated fuel evidence

assurance_standards_or_concepts: ISO 14064-1 direct vs indirect emissions classification
internal_dependencies: Evidence type library J2-002 routing support

assurance_basis: ISO 14064-1 direct vs indirect emissions classification
internal_basis: Evidence type library J2-002 routing support

formula_records.formula_logic: Determine whether portable equipment using retail-purchased fuel should be excluded under Part 253 exemption logic.
revision_summary: Reviewed against formula audit report or existing framework; no structural change applied.

formula_records.formula_logic: Identify fire suppression systems/equipment as excluded where applicable.
revision_summary: Reviewed against formula audit report or existing framework; no structural change applied.

formula_records.formula_logic: Exclude or reclassify municipal/third-party wastewater treatment from facility Scope 1 unless criteria state otherwise.
revision_summary: Reviewed against formula audit report or existing framework; no structural change applied.

formula_records.formula_logic: Exclude or reclassify outsourced logistics outside direct facility Scope 1 unless ownership/control is proven.
revision_summary: Reviewed against formula audit report or existing framework; no structural change applied.

formula_records.formula_logic: If evidence is supporting-only, block automatic normalized activity data unless auditor approves or linked evidence provides activity quantity.
revision_summary: Framed as internal evidence-readiness control; added auditor override trigger and supporting-only reason output.
Added auditor override and linked primary-evidence inputs required by routing exceptions.
Classified as an internal workflow rule and excluded from the regulatory formula table; external/assurance support remains documented separately.
Consolidated duplicate supporting-evidence-as-primary triggers under supporting_only_evidence_used_as_primary_activity.
Moved assertion tags to represented_assertions because this is an internal evidence-routing gate, not a direct assurance test.

branch_conditions.condition: evidence type default disallows downstream activity
branch_conditions.action: block activity creation unless linked primary evidence or auditor override exists

formula_records.formula_logic: Permit activity generation only if required fields are present or auditor-approved exception exists.
revision_summary: Separated internal minimum-field gate from regulatory support; added exception approver input and missing exception/required-field triggers.
Made exception approval conditional rather than universally required and added reason/documentation inputs.
Classified as an internal workflow rule and excluded from the regulatory formula table; external/assurance support remains documented separately.
Moved assertion tags to represented_assertions because this is an internal evidence-routing gate, not a direct assurance test.

branch_conditions.condition: no required fields are missing
branch_conditions.action: allow activity generation

branch_conditions.condition: required fields are missing but an exception is requested
branch_conditions.action: require approver, reason, and documentation before allowing

formula_records.formula_logic: Include mobile fuel only if vehicle/asset is owned or controlled by reporting entity or mapped to facility boundary.
Determine whether refrigerant source category/device applicability applies before calculation.

formula_records.formula_name: Onsite vs third-party treatment boundary check
formula_records.formula_logic: Determine if treatment is onsite/controlled or municipal/third-party; route third-party treatment outside facility Scope 1 unless criteria state otherwise.

formula_records.formula_name: Fossil vs biogenic fuel stream separation
formula_records.regulatory_scope: Part 253 biomass-derived fuel reporting / ISO biogenic treatment
formula_records.formula_logic: Separate fossil and biogenic fuel streams before aggregation; classify blended fuels by documented blend ratio/composition.
Check that biogenic CO2 is identified separately where required and not mixed into fossil Scope 1 totals without disclosure.
Validate biomass-derived fuels are handled under correct Part 253 reporting logic and documented method/factor basis.
Validate CEMS source, method, period, measurement coverage, and QA/QC support before using CEMS data.

formula_records.formula_name: Scope 1 vs Scope 2 energy classification control
formula_records.formula_logic: Purchased electricity supports Scope 2/imported energy or facility-level energy reporting, not Scope 1 combustion, unless tied to onsite fuel/cogeneration.
revision_summary: Replaced generic classification_error with energy-type ambiguity trigger and added accuracy assertion.
Added cogeneration/origin context so classification exceptions can be detected directly.

branch_conditions.condition: energy is purchased/imported electricity
branch_conditions.action: classify as Scope 2 or facility energy input support

branch_conditions.condition: energy is tied to onsite fuel use or cogeneration
branch_conditions.action: route the fuel activity to the applicable Scope 1 calculation and retain electricity as output/support

formula_records.regulatory_scope: Large emission source product-data reporting readiness
formula_records.formula_logic: If facility is a large emission source and applicable industrial sector, check whether product data is complete and reconcilable.

regulatory_basis: NY Part 253 source-category methods and referenced 40 CFR Part 98 subparts

revision_summary: Added applicable Part 253 section input and Part 98 subpart identification trigger.
Added applicable Part 98 subpart input required by the missing-subpart trigger.

branch_conditions.action: no conversion required

branch_conditions.condition: activity unit differs but approved traceable conversion exists
branch_conditions.action: convert using approved conversion factor

revision_summary: Added biogenic CO2 treatment trigger for biomass-derived fuels.

branch_conditions.action: validate separate biogenic CO2 treatment in addition to required gases

Added factor unit and retained source-document identity required by documentation triggers.

formula_records.formula_logic: Check heat content/HHV is available where required by selected stationary combustion method.
revision_summary: Added selected Part 98 tier method input and default-HHV-without-method-basis trigger.
Added HHV type/source inputs required to distinguish default from measured or substitute HHV.
Added explicit HHV-method branches so default HHV is not accepted without an allowed tier/method basis.

branch_conditions.action: require hhv_or_heat_content, hhv_type, and hhv_source_reference; reject an unsupported default HHV

branch_conditions.action: route to the applicable non-HHV method and do not force an HHV input

review_acceptance_notes: Checked against Formula Logic Review practical trigger/citation recommendations in v0.3.1.

Added exclusion/status inputs required to distinguish missing evidence from supported exclusions.

revision_summary: Added same-source/different-document overlap trigger and clarified as assurance-derived cutoff/accuracy control.
Added document identity and account/meter context required by cross-document overlap triggers.

formula_records.formula_logic: If document crosses reporting-year boundary, require allocation method/support before in-scope quantity is used.
revision_summary: Added allocation basis document reference and invoice-date-vs-service-period trigger.
Added date-type and invoice-date inputs required to detect invoice-date substitution for service periods.
Changed allocation_method and allocation_basis_document_reference from universal requirements to conditional cross-year inputs, preventing same-year records from failing unnecessarily.
Replaced broad annual-reporting wording with NY Part 253 §253-1.5(d) and 40 CFR Part 98 §98.3.

branch_conditions.condition: period_start and period_end fall in different reporting years
branch_conditions.action: require allocation_method and allocation_basis_document_reference before using the in-scope quantity

branch_conditions.condition: period_start and period_end fall within the same reporting year
branch_conditions.action: do not require allocation inputs; continue with normalized service period

input_definitions.notes: Required only when period_start and period_end cross the reporting-year boundary.
Required only when cross-year allocation is performed; retains the allocation support for audit traceability.

formula_records.formula_logic: z_score = (value - mean) / standard_deviation
rolling_variance_percent = (current_value - rolling_average) / rolling_average * 100

regulatory_basis: Applicable 40 CFR Part 98 calculation and reporting procedures

Added reporting year and factor-table/version inputs; moved GWP-jurisdiction trigger to Q-010 where the detecting inputs exist.

branch_conditions.condition: factor year/version/table is applicable to fuel, source category, tier, and reporting year
branch_conditions.action: accept; otherwise flag

formula_records.formula_logic: Check GWP basis is documented and consistent across CH4, N2O, and refrigerant CO2e conversions.
revision_summary: Added jurisdictional GWP basis and explicit NY GWP20 vs federal GWP100 triggers.
Added numeric GWP and reference-library inputs so basis/value consistency and refrigerant lookup triggers can execute.
Consolidated duplicate federal-GWP triggers into federal_gwp100_used_for_ny_part253_result and added explicit NY-versus-federal jurisdiction branches.

branch_conditions.condition: jurisdictional_gwp_basis equals NY_PART_253
branch_conditions.action: validate gas_type and gwp_numeric_value against the 6 NYCRR §496.5 GWP20 reference library

branch_conditions.condition: jurisdictional_gwp_basis is federal or another jurisdiction
branch_conditions.action: validate against that jurisdictional library and keep the result separate from NY Part 253 output

formula_records.regulatory_scope: Data quality split for reporting and missing data procedures
formula_records.formula_logic: Summarize actual vs estimated vs substituted portions by quantity, period, and emissions impact.
revision_summary: Added substituted emissions percent output and missing-data threshold/method retention triggers.
Added emissions totals, threshold reference, and method reference needed to calculate substituted-emissions percentage and related triggers.

formula_records.regulatory_scope: NY Part 253 253-2.7 / 40 CFR Part 98 stationary combustion pathway
formula_records.formula_logic: fuel_energy = fuel_quantity * heat_content_or_hhv
formula_records.notes: Tier 4 CEMS units should be routed away from fuel_quantity × HHV energy calculation.
revision_summary: Added calculation tier, HHV type output, tier-appropriate HHV readiness, and Tier 2/Tier 4 gap triggers.
Removed cutoff and responsibility assertions because those are resolved upstream; added HHV source traceability input.

branch_conditions.condition: selected tier requires fuel quantity multiplied by an allowed HHV
branch_conditions.action: calculate fuel energy

branch_conditions.condition: calculation_tier is Tier 4
branch_conditions.action: do not run this formula; route to CEMS method

formula_records.formula_logic: co2_emissions = fuel_energy × co2_emission_factor, with required kg-to-metric-ton conversion where the selected 40 CFR Part 98 equation/factor basis requires it.
formula_records.notes: co2_unit must be validated as metric tons, not free-text.
revision_summary: Added calculation tier, metric-ton conversion trigger, approved factor source trigger, and explicit Table C-1 basis.
Removed duplicated fuel-factor check (owned by V-002) and added factor-source plus metric-ton conversion inputs needed by remaining triggers.

branch_conditions.condition: selected tier uses a prescribed default CO2 factor
branch_conditions.action: apply approved factor and metric-ton conversion

branch_conditions.condition: selected tier uses carbon-content-based calculation
branch_conditions.action: route to the tier-specific carbon-content formula rather than a Table C-1 default factor

formula_records.formula_logic: ch4_emissions = fuel_energy * ch4_emission_factor
revision_summary: Added fuel type input, optional combustion unit type, conditional unit-type factor check, and RICE/turbine natural gas factor triggers.
Added factor identity/source inputs and explicit natural-gas unit-type branch ownership.

branch_conditions.condition: natural-gas unit is not a RICE/turbine exception or another fuel uses the standard factor path
branch_conditions.action: apply the approved standard CH4 factor

branch_conditions.condition: fuel_type is natural gas and combustion_unit_type is RICE or turbine subject to the referenced exception
branch_conditions.action: select the unit-type-specific CH4 factor; do not use the generic Table C-2 default

formula_records.formula_logic: n2o_emissions = fuel_energy * n2o_emission_factor
revision_summary: Added fuel type as explicit required input so fuel-factor matching can execute.
Added factor identity/source inputs so factor selection is traceable.

formula_records.formula_logic: Convert CO2, CH4, and N2O mass to CO2e using the engagement jurisdictional GWP basis; for NY Part 253, validate against the 6 NYCRR §496.5 GWP20 reference record.
formula_records.notes: For NY Part 253 CO2e, validate declared GWP basis and numeric GWP values against the NY GWP20 basis configured for the engagement.
revision_summary: Added GWP basis identifier, GWP20 AR6/NY basis validation, and numeric GWP consistency trigger.
Added GWP reference library input so declared basis and numeric GWP values can be compared.
Replaced generic GWP20/AR6 wording with an explicit jurisdictional branch: NY Part 253 uses the configured 6 NYCRR §496.5 GWP20 reference, while federal GWP logic remains separate.

branch_conditions.condition: jurisdictional_gwp_basis equals NY_PART_253
branch_conditions.action: select and validate the 6 NYCRR §496.5 GWP20 reference record; do not substitute federal Part 98 GWP100 values

branch_conditions.condition: jurisdictional_gwp_basis is not NY_PART_253
branch_conditions.action: route to the separately configured jurisdictional GWP basis; do not reuse the NY result silently

input_definitions.notes: Engagement jurisdiction/reporting basis; NY Part 253 results require the NY GWP20 reference basis.

formula_records.formula_logic: Sum calculated emissions by source, fuel, month, and reporting year; preserve gas-level and fuel-level breakdowns.
revision_summary: Added combustion unit type and reporting year to enforce same-unit-type annual aggregation.
Removed duplicate/missing-period/facility/source checks owned upstream and limited aggregation to dimensions it directly tests.

branch_conditions.condition: all records in an aggregation group share the same combustion_unit_type
branch_conditions.action: aggregate by facility/source/fuel/year while retaining gas-level breakdowns

formula_records.formula_logic: mobile_combustion_co2e = fuel_volume * fuel_specific_emission_factor * gwp_adjustment_or_factor_basis
refrigerant_emitted = beginning_inventory + purchases - ending_inventory - recovered_or_returned_or_sent_offsite
refrigerant_co2e = refrigerant_emitted_mass * gwp_of_refrigerant
refrigerant_emitted_approx = amount_added_during_maintenance; refrigerant_co2e_approx = refrigerant_emitted_approx * gwp_of_refrigerant

formula_records.regulatory_scope: NY Part 253 253-2.2 / wastewater and liquid waste
formula_records.formula_logic: organic_load = wastewater_or_liquid_waste_volume * cod_or_bod_concentration

formula_records.formula_name: Methane emissions from anaerobic digestion / liquid waste
formula_records.formula_logic: ch4_emissions = (organic_load * methane_generation_potential * methane_conversion_factor) - methane_recovered_or_destroyed
wastewater_or_ad_co2e = ch4_emissions * gwp_ch4
process_emissions = total_stack_emissions - combustion_emissions
Aggregate energy inputs by facility, energy type, and reporting period.
Aggregate facility-level energy output if onsite generation/cogeneration exists.
Aggregate annual product quantity by facility, product, unit, and reporting year.
emissions_intensity = annual_emissions / annual_product_quantity
fuel_card_total_volume = sum(vehicle_level_transaction_volumes)
estimated_fuel_volume = general_ledger_fuel_expense / average_fuel_price; compare estimated volume to fuel-card volume.
absolute_difference = recalculated_value - reported_value
revision_summary: Reviewed against formula audit report or existing framework; no structural change applied.

internal_dependencies: Company reported result and system recalculation

internal_basis: Company reported result and system recalculation

formula_records.formula_logic: percent_difference = absolute_difference / reported_value * 100
revision_summary: Reviewed against formula audit report or existing framework; no structural change applied.
Removed difference_exceeds_materiality because R-002 has no materiality threshold; R-003 owns that comparison.

formula_records.formula_logic: Normalize materiality_threshold to the same percentage basis as percent_difference, then set materiality_exceeded_flag = abs(percent_difference) > normalized_materiality_threshold_percent.
revision_summary: Added NY material misstatement and ISO 14064-3 basis while retaining auditor-configured materiality threshold dependency.
Added threshold-unit input and normalized comparison to avoid decimal-versus-percent mismatch; moved materiality trigger here from R-002.
Preserved the auditor-configured materiality threshold as an internal dependency while retaining NY Part 253 §253-1.3(255) as the external regulatory basis.

branch_conditions.condition: materiality_threshold_unit is fraction
branch_conditions.action: convert threshold to percent before comparison

branch_conditions.condition: materiality_threshold_unit is percent
branch_conditions.action: compare directly to percent_difference

formula_records.formula_name: Monthly invoice vs annual meter total reconciliation
formula_records.formula_logic: invoice_meter_variance = sum(monthly_invoice_totals) - annual_meter_total

formula_records.formula_name: Utility bill vs internal energy management system reconciliation
formula_records.formula_logic: utility_ems_variance = utility_bill_quantity - ems_quantity

formula_records.formula_name: Fuel purchase invoice vs accounting record reconciliation
formula_records.formula_logic: fuel_gl_variance = fuel_invoice_total - accounting_record_total
summary_variance = sum(transaction_volumes) - fuel_card_summary_total
Compare service records to purchases, beginning/ending inventory, recovery/disposal records.
Compare volume/load/treatment/capture records for consistency before methane calculation.
variance = current_year_value - prior_year_or_benchmark_value; variance_percent = variance / prior_year_or_benchmark_value * 100
product_data_misstatement_percent = (product_data_discrepancies + product_data_omissions + product_data_misreporting) / total_reported_product_data * 100
emissions_misstatement_percent = (discrepancies + omissions + misreporting) / total_reported_emissions * 100
revision_summary: Removed threshold_status_affected because threshold impact belongs in M-007, not misstatement percentage calculation.
Added explicit regulatory misstatement threshold input so the materiality flag and trigger are executable.

formula_records.formula_logic: product_data_misstatement_percent = (product_data_discrepancies + product_data_omissions + product_data_misreporting) / total_reported_product_data * 100

formula_records.formula_name: Missing data percentage for single data element
formula_records.formula_logic: missing_data_percent = missing_count_or_period / total_required_count_or_period * 100
revision_summary: Reviewed against formula audit report or existing framework; no structural change applied.
Added missing-data method reference required to detect undocumented methods.

formula_records.formula_logic: substituted_emissions_percent = substituted_emissions / total_facility_emissions * 100
revision_summary: Reviewed against formula audit report or existing framework; no structural change applied.
Added substitution method reference required to detect unsupported substitution.

formula_records.formula_logic: Compare annual_facility_co2e against 10,000 metric tons CO2e reporting threshold.
revision_summary: Reviewed against formula audit report or existing framework; no structural change applied.
Removed omitted-source threshold impact trigger; M-007 owns correction/threshold sensitivity.

branch_conditions.condition: annual facility CO2e is compared with the configured 10,000 MT threshold
branch_conditions.action: return below/at/above and distance

formula_records.formula_logic: Compare annual_facility_co2e against 25,000 metric tons CO2e large-emission-source threshold.
revision_summary: Reviewed against formula audit report or existing framework; no structural change applied.

branch_conditions.condition: annual facility CO2e is compared with the configured 25,000 MT threshold
branch_conditions.action: return below/at/above and distance

formula_records.formula_logic: Compare reported and corrected_or_potential emissions separately against the reporting threshold and large-emission-source threshold; return whether either status changes.
revision_summary: Added explicit reporting threshold and large-emission-source threshold citations.
Replaced generic threshold input with separate reporting and large-source regulatory thresholds.
Retained only the exact NY Part 253 §253-1.2(c) and §253-1.2(f) threshold citations as external regulatory basis.

branch_conditions.condition: reported and corrected emissions fall on different sides of either threshold
branch_conditions.action: flag the corresponding threshold status change

architecture_rules: A readiness check or gap trigger may only be owned by a record that declares every input required to evaluate it.
Checks already resolved upstream are represented as dependencies/preconditions and are not repeated as downstream assertion tests.
Every input is typed as document-extracted, system-supplied, upstream output, registry input, reference-library input, company-reported input, auditor/workflow input, or other pipeline context.
Regulatory citations are separated from assurance standards and internal product dependencies.
Regulatory forks based on tier, unit type, jurisdictional GWP basis, source category, or evidence role are represented as branch conditions.
Gap triggers are structured review signals; final auditor conclusions remain human decisions.
The legacy regulatory_basis field contains external regulatory citations only; ISO/ISSA assurance support and internal product dependencies use separate fields.
GapTicket generation records represent upstream assertion findings but do not directly test those assertions again.
NY Part 253 CO2e calculations select the jurisdictional GWP20 reference under 6 NYCRR §496.5; federal GWP100 logic is a separate branch and cannot be substituted silently.
Conditional inputs, such as cross-year allocation support, are required only when the branch condition applies.

pipeline_sequence.purpose: route evidence and block out-of-scope activity generation
validate boundary, method, factor, unit, gas coverage, and traceability prerequisites
completeness, duplication, overlap, unit, factor-version, GWP, and substitution tests
compare system recalculation with reported results
convert upstream findings into auditor-facing gap candidates

review_documents: NY Part 253 Scope 1 Formula Audit Report.pdf
Audit Summary_NY Part 253 Scope 1 Formula Audit Report.docx
integration_policy: A readiness check or trigger must declare the inputs required to detect it.
Gap-ticket rules consume upstream findings and do not re-perform the underlying assertion test.
Document-extracted, system-supplied, upstream, registry, reference-library, company-reported, and workflow inputs remain explicitly typed.
regulatory_basis contains external regulatory citations only; assurance_basis and internal_basis are separate.
NY Part 253 GWP20 logic is distinct from federal Part 98 GWP logic.
Internal evidence-routing and GapTicket rules are excluded from the regulatory formula table.

basis_field_policy.regulatory_basis: External regulatory citations only. Retained for backward compatibility.
basis_field_policy.assurance_basis: ISO/ISSA assurance support; not presented as law or regulation.
basis_field_policy.internal_basis: Product schemas, registries, libraries, and workflow dependencies.
basis_field_policy.broad_references_pending_clause_mapping: Non-specific references retained as pending work and excluded from regulatory_basis.
